fab_section_md:
- The king reigned as a boy under regents; his court was called Aegon III's court (fab-the-lysene-spring-24-p01:67)
- He could not be persuaded even by his brother to come see his sister fly her dragon (fab-the-lysene-spring-24-p01:87)
- When his cupbearer Gaemon was poisoned he was left inconsolable and sank back into gloom (fab-the-lysene-spring-24-p01:119)
- He barred all dragon's eggs from his castle after the monstrous hatching (fab-the-lysene-spring-24-p01:103)
- When the coup came he defended Maegor's Holdfast, disavowing Marston Waters (fab-the-lysene-spring-24-p01:225)
identity_line: Aegon III Targaryen — The king reigned as a boy under regents; his court was called Aegon III's court.

fab_section_md:
- The king's clever, gallant brother, a boy of thirteen who the court doted upon (fab-the-lysene-spring-24-p01:47)
- He announced Larra was with child and later fathered a son by her (fab-the-lysene-spring-24-p01:15)
- He kept his own dragon's egg through exile and captivity and refused to speak to the king for a moon after being forced to yield it (fab-the-lysene-spring-24-p01:103)
- He barred the drawbridge of Maegor's Holdfast with a battleaxe to defend his wife (fab-the-lysene-spring-24-p01:217)
identity_line: Viserys Targaryen — The king's clever, gallant brother, a boy of thirteen who the court doted upon.

fab_section_md:
- Prince Viserys's Lysene wife, who remained a lady of Lys in her heart and would not learn the Common Tongue (fab-the-lysene-spring-24-p01:49)
- She kept her own Lysene gods and rites (fab-the-lysene-spring-24-p01:51)
- Dark rumors held that she could turn into a cat and visited brothels as a man; according to the whispers her worship fed such tales (fab-the-lysene-spring-24-p01:53)
- She gave birth to Viserys's firstborn son at the age of twenty (fab-the-lysene-spring-24-p01:91)
identity_line: Larra Rogare — Prince Viserys's Lysene wife, who remained a lady of Lys in her heart and would not learn the Common Tongue.

fab_section_md:
- Lady Rhaena, wife of Ser Corwyn Corbray and twin of Lady Baela, first flew her dragon Morning at nineteen (fab-the-lysene-spring-24-p01:85)
- She carried Morning to Dragonstone, saying dragons were more welcome there (fab-the-lysene-spring-24-p01:87)
- After her husband's death her twin came to guard against her seeking vengeance (fab-the-lysene-spring-24-p01:137)
identity_line: Rhaena Targaryen — Lady Rhaena, wife of Ser Corwyn Corbray and twin of Lady Baela, first flew her dragon Morning at nineteen.

fab_section_md:
- Lady Baela, twin of Rhaena and wife of Lord Alyn, returned to Driftmark with her husband and their daughter (fab-the-lysene-spring-24-p01:15)
- She went to Dragonstone to comfort her widowed twin (fab-the-lysene-spring-24-p01:137)
identity_line: Baela Targaryen — Lady Baela, twin of Rhaena and wife of Lord Alyn, returned to Driftmark with her husband and their daughter.

fab_section_md:
- Lord Oakenfist refused to join House Lannister's vengeance on the ironmen (fab-the-lysene-spring-24-p01:21)
- He tore the monstrous wyrm off his infant daughter Laena and hacked it apart (fab-the-lysene-spring-24-p01:101)
- He commanded the Velaryon fleet against Gulltown for the Hand (fab-the-lysene-spring-24-p01:137)
identity_line: Alyn Velaryon — Lord Oakenfist refused to join House Lannister's vengeance on the ironmen.

fab_section_md:
- Lord Rowan, Hand and regent, tried to relieve the realm's woes but was obeyed by none (fab-the-lysene-spring-24-p01:43)
- Well-liked but not feared, he was whispered against as having sold himself to the Lyseni (fab-the-lysene-spring-24-p01:43)
- His young wife Lady Floris died in childbirth (fab-the-lysene-spring-24-p01:117)
- He was removed from office and taken into captivity during the coup (fab-the-lysene-spring-24-p01:209)
identity_line: Thaddeus Rowan — Lord Rowan, Hand and regent, tried to relieve the realm's woes but was obeyed by none.

fab_section_md:
- Ser Corwyn, husband of Lady Rhaena, took a council seat after Mooton's death (fab-the-lysene-spring-24-p01:15)
- He ruled for Jeyne's will and declared Joffrey the rightful Lord of the Eyrie (fab-the-lysene-spring-24-p01:123)
- Drawing Lady Forlorn at Runestone, he was shot through the breast by a crossbowman (fab-the-lysene-spring-24-p01:123)
- He was uncle to Quenton Corbray and by marriage kin to Alyn Oakenfist (fab-the-lysene-spring-24-p01:127)
identity_line: Corwyn Corbray — Ser Corwyn, husband of Lady Rhaena, took a council seat after Mooton's death.

fab_section_md:
- The Maiden of the Vale died at Gulltown of a chill at forty, in the arms of Jessamyn Redfort (fab-the-lysene-spring-24-p01:31)
- On her deathbed she named her cousin Ser Joffrey Arryn her heir (fab-the-lysene-spring-24-p01:31)
identity_line: Jeyne Arryn — The Maiden of the Vale died at Gulltown of a chill at forty, in the arms of Jessamyn Redfort.

fab_section_md:
- Ser Joffrey served ten years as Knight of the Bloody Gate before being named heir (fab-the-lysene-spring-24-p01:31)
- Only a fourth cousin, his claim was contested by nearer kin (fab-the-lysene-spring-24-p01:33)
identity_line: Joffrey Arryn — Ser Joffrey served ten years as Knight of the Bloody Gate before being named heir.

fab_section_md:
- Jeyne's first cousin, "the Mad Heir," had twice tried to depose her and was driven mad by long imprisonment (fab-the-lysene-spring-24-p01:33)
- He escaped Corbray and fled to Runestone where he had squired as a boy (fab-the-lysene-spring-24-p01:123)
identity_line: Arnold Arryn — Jeyne's first cousin, "the Mad Heir," had twice tried to depose her and was driven mad by long imprisonment.

fab_section_md:
- Patriarch of the rich Gulltown Arryns, known as the Gilded Falcon, he pressed a claim with gold (fab-the-lysene-spring-24-p01:39)
- He bribed lesser lords and brought sellswords across the sea (fab-the-lysene-spring-24-p01:39)
identity_line: Isembard Arryn — Patriarch of the rich Gulltown Arryns, known as the Gilded Falcon, he pressed a claim with gold.

fab_section_md:
- Lady Johanna sought to put every ironman to the sword in vengeance for the westerlands (fab-the-lysene-spring-24-p01:25)
- According to the narration she swayed the old Sea Lion, mayhaps by promising to wed him (fab-the-lysene-spring-24-p01:21)
- She had the captive Rodrik Greyjoy gelded and made her son's fool (fab-the-lysene-spring-24-p01:27)
identity_line: Johanna Lannister — Lady Johanna sought to put every ironman to the sword in vengeance for the westerlands.

fab_section_md:
- The old Sea Lion landed thousands of Lannister swords on the Iron Islands (fab-the-lysene-spring-24-p01:21)
- He was cut down on Great Wyk by Arthur Goodbrother, and most of his ships were lost (fab-the-lysene-spring-24-p01:21)
identity_line: Leo Costayne — The old Sea Lion landed thousands of Lannister swords on the Iron Islands.

fab_section_md:
- Father of Larra and her brothers, he rose to rule Lys as First Magister for Life under a "velvet tyranny" (fab-the-lysene-spring-24-p01:83)
- His ties to the Iron Throne made the Rogares princes of Lys in all but name (fab-the-lysene-spring-24-p01:63)
- He drowned when his barge sank, one day apart from his brother (fab-the-lysene-spring-24-p01:169)
- He had sired six trueborn sons, three daughters, and sixteen bastards (fab-the-lysene-spring-24-p01:171)
identity_line: Lysandro Rogare — Father of Larra and her brothers, he rose to rule Lys as First Magister for Life under a "velvet tyranny".

fab_section_md:
- Brother of Lysandro and architect of the Dornish alliance, he wed Princess Aliandra Martell and was named Prince Consort and Lord of the Stepstones (fab-the-lysene-spring-24-p01:83)
- He died at Sunspear, choking on a piece of bacon (fab-the-lysene-spring-24-p01:169)
identity_line: Drazenko Rogare — Brother of Lysandro and architect of the Dornish alliance, he wed Princess Aliandra Martell and was named Prince Consort and Lord of the Stepstones.

fab_section_md:
- Lysandro's eldest son took effective control of the Rogare Bank (fab-the-lysene-spring-24-p01:179)
- Said to have twice his father's ambition and half his ability, he bought a thousand Unsullied and diverted the bank's gold (fab-the-lysene-spring-24-p01:181)
- His scheme collapsed and he fled Lys in the night, leaving the bank a hollow shell (fab-the-lysene-spring-24-p01:193)
identity_line: Lysaro Rogare — Lysandro's eldest son took effective control of the Rogare Bank.

fab_section_md:
- Larra's brother commanded her guards and bore a Valyrian steel longsword called Truth (fab-the-lysene-spring-24-p01:143)
- Given command of the Vale army despite poor Common Tongue, his appointment was deeply unpopular (fab-the-lysene-spring-24-p01:145)
- When his translator was slain in Gulltown he could not command his troops (fab-the-lysene-spring-24-p01:149)
- He later yielded his command and took ship for Braavos (fab-the-lysene-spring-24-p01:203)
identity_line: Moredo Rogare — Larra's brother commanded her guards and bore a Valyrian steel longsword called Truth.

fab_section_md: - He was arrested by the gold cloaks as he tried to leave King's Landing (fab-the-lysene-spring-24-p01:203)

- A towering mute from the fighting pits of Meereen, given to Larra by her father Lysandro (fab-the-lysene-spring-24-p01:247)
- His lips and tongue had been cut away, leaving him mute (fab-the-lysene-spring-24-p01:249)
- At Maegor's Holdfast he slew Ser Amaury Peake's men and finally the knight himself with the prince's axe (fab-the-lysene-spring-24-p01:263)
identity_line: Sandoq the Shadow — A towering mute from the fighting pits of Meereen, given to Larra by her father Lysandro.

fab_section_md:
- The bastard boy "King Cunny" served Aegon III as cupbearer, food taster and friend (fab-the-lysene-spring-24-p01:113)
- He was poisoned by tainted apple tarts and died within the hour, thought but nine (fab-the-lysene-spring-24-p01:113)
identity_line: Gaemon Palehair — The bastard boy "King Cunny" served Aegon III as cupbearer, food taster and friend.

fab_section_md:
- The Grand Maester declared the supper poisoned and saved the queen with a purgative (fab-the-lysene-spring-24-p01:111)
- He determined the poison had been baked into the apple tarts (fab-the-lysene-spring-24-p01:115)
- He named the era the "Rogare Ascendency," a term used only at Oldtown (fab-the-lysene-spring-24-p01:69)
identity_line: Munkun — The Grand Maester declared the supper poisoned and saved the queen with a purgative.

fab_section_md: - Ser Amaury came to Maegor's Holdfast to seize Larra for questioning (fab-the-lysene-spring-24-p01:219)
identity_line: Amaury Peake — Ser Amaury came to Maegor's Holdfast to seize Larra for questioning.

fab_section_md: - The Kingsguard Lord Commander was made the new King's Hand after Rowan's removal (fab-the-lysene-spring-24-p01:221)
identity_line: Marston Waters — The Kingsguard Lord Commander was made the new King's Hand after Rowan's removal.

fab_section_md:
- Lord Thaddeus's eldest son led the host up the kingsroad into the Mountains of the Moon (fab-the-lysene-spring-24-p01:131)
- He was crushed by a falling boulder toppled by the clansmen (fab-the-lysene-spring-24-p01:163)
identity_line: Robert Rowan — Lord Thaddeus's eldest son led the host up the kingsroad into the Mountains of the Moon.

fab_section_md:
- "Bloody Ben" led a thousand Blackwoods and had much experience of war despite his youth (fab-the-lysene-spring-24-p01:163)
- He assumed command of the host after Robert Rowan's death (fab-the-lysene-spring-24-p01:163)
identity_line: Benjicot Blackwood — "Bloody Ben" led a thousand Blackwoods and had much experience of war despite his youth.

fab_section_md: - The Bronze Giant of Runestone, old and stubborn, rode out in bronze armor to confront Corbray (fab-the-lysene-spring-24-p01:123)
identity_line: Gunthor Royce — The Bronze Giant of Runestone, old and stubborn, rode out in bronze armor to confront Corbray.

fab_section_md: - The Princess of Dorne wed Drazenko Rogare and named him Prince Consort and Lord of the Stepstones (fab-the-lysene-spring-24-p01:83)
identity_line: Aliandra Martell — The Princess of Dorne wed Drazenko Rogare and named him Prince Consort and Lord of the Stepstones.

fab_section_md:
- The king's little queen was poisoned alongside Gaemon but saved by Munkun's purgative (fab-the-lysene-spring-24-p01:111)
- She was so delighted by Morning that she wished for a dragon of her own (fab-the-lysene-spring-24-p01:87)
identity_line: Daenaera Velaryon — The king's little queen was poisoned alongside Gaemon but saved by Munkun's purgative.

fab_section_md:
- The boy upon the Seastone Chair, seized by the Red Kraken's sisters, who put his mother to death (fab-the-lysene-spring-24-p01:19)
- He held the Chair when his defenders beat off the Lannister assault on Pyke (fab-the-lysene-spring-24-p01:27)
identity_line: Toron Greyjoy — The boy upon the Seastone Chair, seized by the Red Kraken's sisters, who put his mother to death.
